question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: parseParent()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction with a parentConsensusTimestamp that exactly matches a previous sibling's consensusTimestamp (not the true parent) during a network partition where items arrive out of order, causing parseParent() to resolve the wrong RecordItem as parent and corrupt the parent-child chain stored in the database?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: parseParent()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a child transaction whose parentConsensusTimestamp equals previous.parent.transactionRecord.getParentConsensusTimestamp() (grandparent path) during a partition-induced reorder, causing parseParent() to return previous.parent.parent (a null or wrong ancestor), silently dropping the parent link and making the child appear as a top-level transaction?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: parseSuccess()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that delivers a failed parent RecordItem after its child, so that when parseSuccess() evaluates 'if (parent != null && !parent.isSuccessful()) return false', the parent reference is null (not yet linked), causing a failed child to be recorded as successful and triggering incorrect state changes in the mirror node database?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: parseTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with both signedTransactionBytes and bodyBytes populated during a partition where the stream is replayed with a different encoding path, causing parseTransaction() to parse signedTransactionBytes on first delivery but bodyBytes on replay, producing two RecordItems with the same consensusTimestamp but different transactionBody contents, leading to database inconsistency?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: parseTransactionType()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a TransactionBody with exactly one unknown protobuf field during a network partition that causes the mirror node to re-parse a record file, causing parseTransactionType() to log the unknown field number as the transaction type and persist an incorrect transactionType value, corrupting transaction type metrics and query results?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: parseTransactionType()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a TransactionBody with zero unknown fields and DATA_NOT_SET during a partition-induced duplicate delivery, causing parseTransactionType() to log an error and return DATA_NOT_SET.getNumber() (0), which is then stored as the transaction type, making the transaction invisible to type-filtered queries?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: parsePossiblyHookContractRelatedParent()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a sequence of contract call transactions to HOOK_CONTRACT_NUM (365) during a network partition that reorders siblings, causing parsePossiblyHookContractRelatedParent() to traverse the previous-linked list and identify the wrong sibling as the hookParent, misattributing hook storage changes to the wrong hook execution context?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: parsePossiblyHookContractRelatedParent()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger an unbounded while-loop in parsePossiblyHookContractRelatedParent() by crafting a long chain of previous-linked RecordItems all with non-null ContractFunctionResults and contractNum != HOOK_CONTRACT_NUM during a partition that delivers a large batch, causing CPU exhaustion (denial of service) in the mirror node importer?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: nextHookContext()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that delivers a child CryptoTransfer RecordItem before its parent, so that hookExecutionQueue is null and nextHookContext() delegates to parent.nextHookContext() on a null parent, returning null and causing processHookStorageChanges() to log a recoverable error and skip all hook storage updates for that execution?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: nextHookContext()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a CryptoTransfer with more hook child transactions than entries in the hookExecutionQueue, exploiting a partition-induced partial delivery of the parent's queue setup, causing nextHookContext() to return null for excess children and silently drop hook storage changes from the database?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: build()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a NullPointerException in RecordItemBuilder.build() by submitting a TransactionRecord with a null consensusTimestamp during a network partition where the record file is partially delivered, since DomainUtils.timestampInNanosMax() is called unconditionally on transactionRecord.getConsensusTimestamp() with no null guard?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: build()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that delivers the same RecordItem twice (duplicate delivery) to cause build() to be called twice on the same builder state, resulting in two RecordItems with identical consensusTimestamp values being inserted into the database, violating the primary key uniqueness constraint and causing an importer crash?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: isTopLevel()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction with transactionNonce > 0 and scheduled=true during a network partition that drops the parent record, causing isTopLevel() to return true (via the scheduled nonce branch) for what is actually a child transaction, leading to incorrect top-level processing and duplicate state application?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: isSystemFileUpdate()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a FileUpdate transaction paid by account 0.0.50 during a network partition that causes the shard/realm from CommonProperties.getInstance() to be stale or zero, causing isSystemFileUpdate() to incorrectly classify a non-system file update as a system one, granting it top-level processing status and bypassing child transaction constraints?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getTransactionHash()] Can an unprivileged external user submit an EthereumTransaction where getEthereumTransaction() returns a non-null object with a null hash during a network partition that causes partial EthereumTransaction population, causing getTransactionHash() to return null from EthereumTransaction::getHash and then fail on Arrays.copyOfRange with a NullPointerException?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getTransactionHash()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction whose transactionRecord.getTransactionHash() returns a ByteString shorter than 32 bytes during a network partition that truncates the record, causing Arrays.copyOfRange(DomainUtils.toBytes(...), 0, 32) to return a zero-padded hash that collides with another transaction's hash in the database?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: addContractTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that delivers a child contract call before its parent's contractTransactionPredicate is set (null), causing addContractTransaction() to silently return without recording the contract transaction, resulting in missing ContractTransaction entries in the database for that child?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: addContractTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a contract transaction with an EntityId whose getId() returns a value that already exists as a key in contractTransactions during a partition-induced replay, causing computeIfAbsent() to silently skip the new entry and preserve stale ContractTransaction data from the first delivery?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: doAddEntityId()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that delivers transactions out of order so that entityTransactionBuilder is initialized with the consensusTimestamp and transactionType of the first-processed (wrong) transaction, causing all subsequent addEntityId() calls on the same RecordItem to build EntityTransaction objects with incorrect metadata?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: doAddEntityId()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction referencing a large number of distinct EntityIds during a network partition that causes repeated addEntityId() calls, causing the entityTransactions HashMap to grow unboundedly in memory, leading to an OutOfMemoryError in the importer process (denial of service)?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: setEntityNftTransactionPredicate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that delivers a RecordItem with a null payerAccountId (due to missing TransactionID in the body), causing setEntityNftTransactionPredicate() to call predicate.and(entityId -> !payerAccountId.equals(entityId)) and throw a NullPointerException when payerAccountId is null?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: populateContractTransactions()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a contract transaction that adds a large number of EntityIds to contractTransactions during a network partition that causes the same RecordItem to be processed twice, causing populateContractTransactions() to call setContractIds() with a doubled list of IDs, corrupting the contractIds field of all ContractTransaction objects?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getContractRelatedParent()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that delivers a hook child transaction before its hookParent is resolved, causing getContractRelatedParent() to fall through to the parent.hasContractResult() check on a wrong parent, returning an incorrect contract-related parent and misattributing contract results in the database?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: parseTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction where signedTransactionBytes is non-empty but contains a valid SignedTransaction with empty bodyBytes, causing parseTransaction() to set transactionBody to an empty TransactionBody (not null), bypassing the null check at line 430 and producing a RecordItem with an empty transactionBody that causes NullPointerExceptions in downstream handlers?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: parseTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a SignedTransaction whose bodyBytes parse to a valid TransactionBody but whose sigMap is empty during a network partition that replays the transaction, causing parseTransaction() to accept the empty SignatureMap and store a RecordItem with no signatures, bypassing any downstream signature-count validation?
